root_cause: Both fetchProfile and fetchOrders write to results.requestId on a shared object. Promise.all runs them concurrently. Whichever resolves last wins — making results.requestId non-deterministic. The shared results object is mutated by two concurrent async branches with no coordination.
fix: Remove requestId writes from the individual fetch functions. Decide deterministically which requestId to use (e.g., always profile's) and assign it once after Promise.all resolves.
